result.markdown: --- title: utils.ks.KS_test --- ```python utils.ks.KS_test(series, w_min, w_max, n_w, d_c, n_c, s_c, x_c=None) ``` Detect tipping points using Kolmogorov-Smirnov test Applies sliding window KS test to detect abrupt transitions using the method of Bagniewski et al. (2021). ## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters} [**series**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[ammonyte](`ammonyte`).[Series](`ammonyte.Series`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Series object containing time and value data [**w_min**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of smallest sliding window in time units [**w_max**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Size of largest sliding window in time units [**n_w**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Number of window sizes to test [**d_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Cut-off threshold for KS statistic [**n_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Minimum sample size per window [**s_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Standard deviation ratio threshold [**x_c**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default} : Change threshold. If None, auto-calculated ## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns} [**jumps**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Array of detected transitions with shape (n, 2) Each row: [time, direction] where direction is +1 (up) or -1 (down) [**d_statistics**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Array of KS D-statistics corresponding to each detected transition [**p_values**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation} : Array of p-values corresponding to each detected transition ## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples} Basic usage (typically called via Series.kstest): ::: {#87513840 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import os, ammonyte as amt from ammonyte.utils.ks import KS_test ngrip = amt.Series.from_csv(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(amt.__file__), 'data', 'NGRIP.csv')) transitions = KS_test(ngrip, w_min=0.12, w_max=2.5, n_w=15, d_c=0.77, n_c=3, s_c=2.0, x_c=0.8) print(f"Detected {len(transitions[0])} transitions") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Time axis values sorted in ascending order Time axis values sorted in ascending order Detected 104 transitions ``` ::: ::: ## See Also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also} Series.kstest : High-level interface to this function ## References {.doc-section .doc-section-references} Bagniewski, W., et al. (2021). Automatic detection of abrupt transitions in paleoclimate records. Chaos, 31(11), 113129. [[source]](https://github.com/LinkedEarth/Ammonyte/blob/main/ammonyte/utils/ks.py#L24-L379){.source-link target="_blank"}
